refinement_sections.content: We need to figure out how to change a secondary alcohol into a ketone using a safe oxidizing agent. But we don't know which alcohol or oxidizing agent to use.
Choose a secondary alcohol and an environmentally friendly oxidizing agent to study. For example, we could use isopropanol as the alcohol and hydrogen peroxide as the oxidizing agent.

We should outline the steps of the reaction. First, we need to show how the oxidizing agent interacts with the alcohol. Then, we need to illustrate the formation of the ketone and mention any important intermediates.
Research how the chosen oxidizing agent affects the environment. Discuss if it produces any harmful byproducts and if it is safe to use.
Think about what other reactions might happen besides the main one. Try to list some possible side products and suggest ways to prevent them.
